content.full_text:
October 22, 1991

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812

ATT: Deanne Stone

Dear Ms. Stone,

Per our phone conversation of 10-22-91, please note the following facts:

-I own 4 lots that are unbuildable due to non-perkability in the Meadowmont Subdivision in Arnold.

-The adjacent subdivision, Lakemont Pines, has sent a letter to our water district asking that we provide information to them in regard to hooking up to the Arnold sewer treatment plant. They have a total of 551 lots, of which 359 are improved, with the majority of the balance of 192 lots being unbuildable due to non-perkability. The improved lots are all on septic tanks.

-There are about 100 lots in Meadowmont abutting Lakemont Pines that are likely to want the same access to the sewer treatment plant, 4 of which are mine. Most likely, the project would encompass both areas.

-The value of all unbuildable lots, including mine, would be enhanced with access to sewer.

-CCWD (Calaveras County Water District) serves about 5,000 customers for water in my area, and about 500 for sewer.

I suspect that I would be in conflict if this project gathers any steam, but am unclear on whether I would be receiving a benefit that others would not. Hence, my letter, requesting an opinion, in writing, of whether or not I would have a conflict-of-interest in this matter.

The water district has held off for a number of months on this request from Lakemont Pines, but appears now to be ready to start an informational dialogue. Because of this timeliness, I would ask for you to reply to me ASAP.

Sincerely,

Dick Weinkle
Director of District 3
Calaveras County Water District
